messages.content: This JavaScript code checks if the radio input with ID "j" is checked. If it is checked, it will display an alert saying "Radio input with ID j is checked!". If it is not checked, it will display an alert saying "Radio input with ID j is not checked."
It is not working. Thats my HTML:
It seems like there may be a syntax error in your HTML code. Could you please provide the HTML code so that I can help identify the issue?
<input type=„radio“ id“j“ value=„0“ name=„bewertung“>

I see the issue in your HTML code. The attribute assignment for the "id" attribute is incorrect. It should be using an equal sign (=) instead of a colon. Here is the corrected HTML code:

```html
<input type="radio" id="j" value="0" name="bewertung">
```

Now, with the corrected HTML code, the JavaScript code should work correctly to check if the radio input with ID "j" is checked.
Sorry, I do have it like that.

I apologize for the confusion. It seems like there was a typo in the attribute assignment in your original HTML code. The attribute "id" should have an equal sign (=), not a colon. 
Here is the corrected version of the HTML code you provided:

```html
<input type="radio" id="j" value="0" name="bewertung">
```

With this corrected HTML code, the JavaScript code should work properly to check if the radio input with ID "j" is checked. If you are still facing issues, please let me know, and I'll be happy to help further.
The function is called bewerten() and runs when a button is clicked using onClick
Thats the full HTML and bewerten is not working:
